'''TK-577''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]], holding the position of [[Fire team/Legends|fire team]] leader. By some point following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], TK-577 had deserted the Empire and become one of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild's]] top ten most wanted fugitives. He was ultimately tracked down and [[Death/Legends|killed]] by a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who was pursuing all of the top ten fugitives.

==Biography==
[[File:TK577-SWG.png|thumb|left|150px|TK-577]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="SWG" /> TK-577 served as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]]<ref name="Fire Team Leader" /> in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]<ref name="SWG" /> during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He<ref name="Quick Draw" /> had the position of [[Fire team/Legends|fire team]] leader.<ref name="Fire Team Leader" />

At some point after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], TK-577 deserted the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] and became one of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild's]] top ten most wanted fugitives. A [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 25,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] was placed on his head.<ref name="SWG" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
The former [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] was ultimately tracked down and [[Death/Legends|killed]] by a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] dedicated to hunting down the top ten most wanted fugitives hiding throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. As a result, the bounty hunter received the 25,000 credit bounty.<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
During his Imperial service, TK-577 believed in what he was fighting for as much as any recruit of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="Quick Draw" /> Despite that, he still deserted the Empire following the Battle of Yavin, and became a highly wanted fugitive. As of his [[Time/Legends|time]] as a fugitive, TK-577 was completely bald with [[brown]] [[Hair/Legends|eyebrows]]. His [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were also brown in [[Color/Legends|color]], and he had fair skin.<ref name="SWG" />

==Equipment==
During TK-577's time in the Stormtrooper Corps, he was armed with a [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech]] [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]].<ref name="Fire Team Leader" /> After deserting the Empire, however, TK-577 made use of a [[DL-44 XT]] [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and a [[Vibroknuckler/Legends|vibroknuckler]] for close range combat.<ref name="SWG" />

==Behind the scenes==
TK-577 was created by [[Decipher]]'s ''[[Jedi Knights Trading Card Game]]'' in [[2001]].<ref name="Fire Team Leader" /> He later appeared as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]].<ref name="SWG" /> He was added to the game as part of [[:w:c:swg:Game Update 3|Game Update 3's]] [[:w:c:swg:Dangerous Fugitives|Dangerous Fugitives]] addition in [[April 29]], [[2008]].<ref name="Fugitives">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67706&month=042008|text=Friday Feature – Fugitives}}</ref> The game was shut down on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{WebCite|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/technology/video-games/8601159/Star-Wars-Galaxies-MMORPG-to-close-this-year.html|text=Star Wars Galaxies MMORPG to close this year|date=2011-06-27, 3:42 pm BST|work=The Telegraph|quote=Sony, who created the game, announced via email and the game's support forum and website that the online game will be closed on 15 December [2011].|archivedate=20160324060142}}</ref>

Players of the [[:w:c:swg:Bounty Hunter|bounty hunter]] [[:w:c:swg:Profession|profession]] who were [[:w:c:swg:Combat level|level 86]] or higher could use a bounty check ability to discover ten fugitives hiding throughout the galaxy. One of these fugitives was TK-577. Bounty hunter players who defeated all ten fugitives in the Dangerous Fugitives [[:w:c:swg:Collection|collection]] were awarded with a Master Crafted [[EE-3 carbine rifle/Legends|EE-3 Carbine]] [[:w:c:swg:EE3 Carbine|Schematic]] and a Master Crafted [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15 Rifle]] [[:w:c:swg:DC-15 Rifle|Schematic]]. Players who completed the collection could speak to a [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] bounty hunter named [[Rovessh]] in the [[Bestine (city)/Legends|Bestine]] [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] to reset the collection, allowing players to repeatedly hunt down and kill TK-577 and the other nine fugitives. Although the player could choose not to hunt down and kill TK-577, this article assumes 100% game completion.<ref name="SWG" />
